name: Victor Gomez
summary: 45-year-old male presenting with acute low back pain after heavy lifting, managed conservatively with NSAIDs and physical therapy, resulting in complete resolution.

conditions.name: Acute Low Back Pain
conditions.notes: Likely musculoskeletal strain from heavy lifting.

visits.chief_complaint: Acute low back pain after lifting
visits.narrative: Patient presents with sudden onset low back pain after lifting a heavy box yesterday. Pain is 8/10, localized to lumbar region, non-radiating. No neurological deficits, no bowel/bladder changes. Physical exam shows tenderness to palpation in lumbar paraspinal muscles, limited flexion. Straight leg raise negative. Impression: Acute lumbar strain. Prescribed Ibuprofen 600mg TID. Advised rest, heat/ice, and gentle stretching. Return in 1 week if no improvement.

Patient returns reporting significant improvement in low back pain. Pain is now 2/10, occurring only with specific movements. Has been compliant with Ibuprofen and self-care measures. Physical exam shows mild residual tenderness, full range of motion without pain. Patient feels much better and able to return to normal activities. Impression: Resolved acute lumbar strain. Advised to continue gentle activity, discontinue Ibuprofen as pain resolves. No further follow-up needed unless symptoms recur.
